title: the Information Note regarding "Developments in the EU Legislation on the Green Agreement"
bodyHtml:
The Union of Chambers and Commodity Exchanges of Turkey (TOBB) has released an article regarding "Developments in the EU Legislation on the Green Agreement."

Following the release of the Green Agreement document in 2019, the European Union began to transform all of its industries in line with low-carbon production, circular economy, and sustainability principles, and update its legislation in accordance with the green transformation policy.

Considering that over 40 percent of our country's total exports are made to the European Union, it is inevitable that legislative changes in the European Union will also affect our country's sectors. While our country's legislation is being made compliant with EU legislation by relevant Ministries, harmonization studies are time-consuming processes.

In this context, it is important to monitor the developments in the EU and review the production processes within this framework in order to prevent any disruption in our exports to the EU. Accordingly, the "Information Note on Developments in EU Legislation on the Green Agreement" has been prepared with the contributions of official letters and bulletins from the Ministry of Trade.

Click here for the Information Note prepared by TOBB.
